message: GET serverFn detected. GET requests should be idempotent and safe — they should not create, update, or delete data. If this handler mutates data, change the method to POST to prevent CSRF vulnerabilities via prefetching.

Root route component missing <HeadContent />. TanStack Start requires <HeadContent /> in the root route component to render <title> and other head elements defined by child routes. Without it, meta tags are silently dropped during rendering.

Direct fetch() call inside a TanStack Start route loader. Loaders run on the client during navigation; using fetch() directly creates client-side waterfalls and bypasses server-side data fetching. Wrap the call in createServerFn() so it runs on the server with full Node.js access.
Dynamic import() inside a serverFn handler or route. Dynamic imports of serverFn break the RPC stub that TanStack Start generates at build time. Use a static import at the top of the file instead.

navigate() called in component render body instead of a route loader or user event handler. Call redirect() in the route's loader for declarative redirects, or place navigate() inside an event handler / useEffect.

navigate() called in component render body instead of a route loader or user event handler. Call redirect() in the route's loader for declarative redirects, or place navigate() inside an event handler / useEffect.

Secret environment variable accessed inside a TanStack Start route loader. Route loaders can be compiled into the client bundle, which would expose the secret. Move secret access into a createServerFn() to guarantee server-only execution.

Secret environment variable accessed inside a TanStack Start route loader. Route loaders can be compiled into the client bundle, which would expose the secret. Move secret access into a createServerFn() to guarantee server-only execution.

"use server" is redundant inside a createServerFn().handler() callback. TanStack Start automatically creates a server boundary at the handler boundary — the directive has no effect here and misleads readers. Remove it.

"use server" is redundant inside a createServerFn().handler() callback. TanStack Start automatically creates a server boundary at the handler boundary — the directive has no effect here and misleads readers. Remove it.

useEffect + fetch() used for data fetching in a TanStack Start route component. TanStack Router provides a loader function that runs before the component renders, eliminating this pattern and preventing client-side waterfalls. Move the fetch call to the route's loader and access data via useLoaderData().

Calling 'redirect()' or 'notFound()' inside a try-catch block can prevent the navigation from working correctly because TanStack Router uses thrown errors internally to redirect or show a 404 page. Call redirect() outside the try-catch block or rethrow the exception.

createServerFn() chain has .handler() before .validator(). The correct order is createServerFn().validator(schema).handler(fn) — calling .handler() first means the validator schema is attached after the handler is already set, so input validation does not run.
createServerFn() in this file does not use CSRF middleware. TanStack Start recommends createCsrfMiddleware() to protect against cross-site request forgery on state-changing endpoints.

createServerFn() chain missing .inputValidator(schema). Without input validation, untrusted client data reaches the server function unchecked, opening the route to injection and type-confusion attacks. TanStack Start docs show inputValidator as the standard contract.
